title: ferray-ma: no domain-aware ufunc wrapping (e.g., log of negative masked, sqrt of negative masked)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's masked ufuncs have domain checking: ma.log(masked_array) automatically masks elements that are out of domain (negative values). Similarly, ma.sqrt masks negative inputs, ma.divide masks zeros in the denominator. This is implemented via _DomainCheckInterval, _DomainGreater, _DomainSafeDivide classes in core.py. ferray-ma's ufunc_support applies the function blindly to unmasked elements — log of a negative value produces NaN but doesn't set the mask. Add domain-aware wrapping at least for log, sqrt, divide, arcsin, arccos.
